body:
## Summary

Fixes #479. Desktop apps showed **"Not available in latest release"** on `/download` for every platform. Root cause: the desktop-release workflow resolved the **parent** commit SHA, but the auto-release tag is created on the **child** `chore: release` commit, so the exact-SHA match never succeeded and **v0.187.0–v0.201.0 shipped zero desktop assets**. This PR fixes that, refreshes the obsolete screenshots, adds the macOS install screenshots, restructures the site into a landing → app / docs / download chooser with CI/CD for every surface (including a generated `/docs/api` Rust reference), and records a full case study.

## What changed

### 1. Desktop release availability (the bug)
- New `scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh` with **two-tier resolution**: tier 1 exact-SHA match, tier 2 "latest published release / auto-release child commit". This is the fix that makes desktop assets attach to the right release. Verbose logging added for future diagnosis.
- Guarded by `tests/unit/ci-cd/desktop_release_resolve.rs` (8 tests covering exact-SHA, child-commit, idempotency, dispatch, and skip paths).
- Verified across the whole codebase: the only `workflow_run`/`head_sha` consumer is `desktop-release.yml`, and it now flows through the fixed script — no other instance of the bug shape.

### 2. Obsolete + missing screenshots
- Regenerated the `/download` desktop app-preview and page screenshots.
- Added macOS Gatekeeper install screenshots (mirroring vk-bot-desktop), covered by `tests/e2e/tests/issue-479.spec.js`.

### 3. Site restructure with CI/CD for every surface
```
/            landing-page chooser   (src/web/index.html + landing.{js,css})
/app/        the interactive web app (relocated; <base href="../">)
/docs/       documentation hub       (src/web/docs/ → /docs/api + prose docs)
/docs/api/   Rust API reference       (cargo doc, generated at deploy time)
/download/   desktop download page
```
- The app moves under `/app/` with `<base href="../">` so all relative asset/worker/seed URLs still resolve to the shared root — no asset edits, correct under both Pages' `/formal-ai/` prefix and the desktop static server. The desktop wrapper now opens `/app/index.html`.
- Landing + docs render through a shared `src/web/site-chrome.js` chooser factory: shared Links-Notation preference store (theme + UI language carry across pages), strict same-origin CSP, `<noscript>` fallback, localized into en/ru/zh/hi.
- `release.yml`'s `deploy-demo` job generates the API reference with `cargo doc --no-deps --lib` (+ a root redirect so `/docs/api/` doesn't 404) and copies it into the Pages artifact **after** stamping. Job timeout 15→20m for the cold-cache compile; concurrency made **main-safe** so a release run is never cancelled mid-flight (matching `desktop-release.yml`).
- New `tests/e2e/tests/issue-479-site.spec.js` exercises both chooser pages (nav targets, app boot under base href, language/theme persistence, hidden version label on un-stamped builds), deployment-agnostic so it runs on local **and** Pages configs. `workflow_release.rs` gains guards for the new static + CI invariants.

### 4. CI hygiene (unblocks the Lint job)
- Newer stable Rust promoted two clippy lints to deny-by-default, which were silently failing `cargo clippy --all-targets` under `-D warnings` (masked behind the first error):
  - `src/agentic_coding/lexicon.rs`: `(len + 1) / 2` → `len.div_ceil(2)` (`manual_div_ceil`; `div_ceil` is stable since 1.73, within the crate's 1.77 MSRV — the avoidance comment cited a stale 1.70).
  - `tests/unit/ci-cd/desktop_release_resolve.rs`: backtick `GITHUB_TOKEN`/`GITHUB_OUTPUT` in docs (`doc_markdown`) and `#[allow(clippy::literal_string_with_formatting_args)]` on the mock-gh raw shell string.
- Added the changelog fragment for #479 (`bump: minor`).

### 5. Case study & template comparison
- `docs/case-studies/issue-479/`: timeline reconstruction, full requirement list, root-cause analysis, candidate solutions, surveyed components — plus the downloaded raw logs/data and a side-by-side comparison of the four link-foundation CI/CD templates.
- **No #479-analogous bug exists in any template** (none drive desktop releases via `workflow_run`/`head_sha`); `template-comparison/REPORT.md` enumerates the cross-cutting best practices worth porting upstream. Since the mandatory "report the same bug" trigger is not met and filing on external repos is outward-facing, those enhancements are **documented (not filed)** and ready to open on request.

## Testing — CI green ✅
All PR checks pass: **Lint and Format**, **Test (ubuntu-latest)**, **E2E Tests (local demo)**, **Code Coverage**, **Build Package**, **Changelog Fragment**, **Detect Changes**, **Version Modification**. (Deploy/release jobs are intentionally `main`-only and skip on PRs.)

Locally:
- `cargo test --test unit ci_cd` → **61 passed** (8 desktop-resolve + 2 new restructure guards + existing guards updated for the new layout). Clippy & rustfmt clean.
- Full local Playwright suite → **285 passed**; `cargo doc` verified (emits `target/doc/formal_ai/index.html`). The only local failures are the 5 `issue-221` *live-Wiktionary* tests, which fail **only** in the network-isolated sandbox (HTTP 403 to wiktionary.org) — untouched by this PR and **green in CI's E2E job**.

## Verbatim requirement coverage
✅ Desktop apps available again (root-cause fix) · ✅ refreshed screenshots · ✅ macOS install screenshots · ✅ vk-bot-desktop best practices · ✅ CI/CD for `/`, `/app`, `/docs/*`, `/docs/api`, `/download` · ✅ landing chooser to app/docs/download · ✅ CI/CD template comparison (no analogous bug; enhancements documented) · ✅ case study with downloaded logs/data · ✅ verbose/debug output added · ✅ applied across the whole codebase · ✅ single PR.

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/479

…(#479)

Root cause of "Not available in latest release" for every desktop app:
the automated CI/CD release bumps the version in a NEW child commit
("chore: release vX.Y.Z"), annotates THAT commit with the vX.Y.Z tag, and
creates the GitHub release from it -- all pushed with GITHUB_TOKEN. GitHub
therefore suppresses the `release` event (recursion guard) and never starts
a CI run for the child commit. The "CI/CD Pipeline" run that DOES complete
carries the PARENT commit's SHA.

The Desktop Release `resolve` job required a tag whose commit EQUALS the
workflow_run head SHA. Because the tag lives on the child commit, that match
never succeeded -> the build job was skipped -> no desktop assets were ever
uploaded -> the /download page reported "Not available in latest release"
for all platforms.

Fix: extract the resolve logic into scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh and
resolve the latest published release (the auto-release child) on
workflow_run, with a defensive exact-SHA tier, a diagnostic parent-SHA
confirmation, and a self-healing idempotency guard that skips only when the
release already carries desktop assets. Emit grouped ::group:: diagnostics
for future debugging.

Tests: tests/unit/ci-cd/desktop_release_resolve.rs drives the script against
a mocked gh CLI across 8 event shapes (the auto_release_child_commit case is
the direct #479 reproduction: old logic -> should_build=false, fixed ->
should_build=true). The workflow_release guard is updated to forbid the old
head-SHA skip path from returning.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(desktop-release): build assets for auto-release child-commit tag …

commits.messageBody:
The issue asks for macOS screenshots like konard.github.io/vk-bot-desktop.
Gatekeeper dialogs cannot be triggered on a hosted macOS CI runner, so this
adds three faithful, on-brand reproductions of the macOS 15 (Sequoia)
dialogs rendered from a self-contained fixture and committed as retina PNGs:

  macos-gatekeeper-not-opened.png   -> System Settings step 1
  macos-gatekeeper-open-anyway.png  -> System Settings step 2
  macos-gatekeeper-confirm.png      -> System Settings step 3

- tests/e2e/fixtures/macos-gatekeeper.html: the fixture (3 dialogs)
- tests/e2e/scripts/generate-macos-screenshots.mjs: deterministic generator
- download.js: embed the figure in the System Settings details, with
  localized alt text + caption added to all four locales (en/ru/zh/hi)
- download.css: .install-macos-screenshots grid styling
- tests/e2e/tests/issue-479.spec.js: assert the figures render, the images
  load (naturalWidth > 0), and alt/caption are localized in every language
commits.messageHeadline: feat(download): add macOS Gatekeeper screenshots to install steps (#479)

commits.messageBody:
…ots (#479)

The issue notes the desktop-app screenshots are obsolete. Regenerated via the
issue-347 Playwright suite against the current app:

- app-preview-<locale>-<theme>.png (+ app-preview.png fallback): the in-window
  chat-app preview the /download page layers over its placeholder, refreshed to
  the current UI.
- docs/screenshots/issue-347/download-<locale>-<theme>.png: full /download page
  captures, which now also include the new macOS Gatekeeper screenshots.
commits.messageHeadline: chore(download): refresh obsolete desktop app-preview + page screensh…

commits.messageBody:
…arison (#479)

Captures the full investigation the issue asked for under
docs/case-studies/issue-479/:

- README.md — timeline reconstruction, the complete requirement list, the
  root-cause analysis of "Not available in latest release" (the desktop-release
  resolve step matched the parent commit SHA while the auto-release tag lives on
  the child "chore: release" commit, so no release ever matched and v0.187.0–
  v0.201.0 shipped zero desktop assets), candidate solutions, and the
  components/libraries surveyed.
- raw-data/ — the downloaded logs and API payloads the analysis is built on
  (the failing desktop-release run log + run list, the issue/PR JSON, the
  release asset evidence, the root-cause evidence notes, and the current
  vk-bot-desktop reference App.jsx + macOS screenshots).
- template-comparison/ — a side-by-side of the four link-foundation CI/CD
  templates (js/rust/python/csharp): each template's full file tree, the
  relevant workflows/actions, and REPORT.md. The report confirms no
  #479-analogous bug exists in any template (none drive desktop releases via
  workflow_run/head_sha) and enumerates the cross-cutting CI/CD best practices
  worth porting upstream.
commits.messageHeadline: docs(case-study): add issue-479 root-cause analysis and template comp…

commits.messageBody:
…479)

Issue #479 asks the project to adopt the vk-bot-desktop structure where a
visitor lands on a chooser and selects where to go — the web app, the
documentation, or the desktop download — and to have CI/CD for each surface
(landing /, app /app, docs /docs/* + API reference /docs/api, download
/download).

Site layout (all assets stay at the site root, shared by every page):

  /            landing-page chooser (src/web/index.html + landing.{js,css})
  /app/        the interactive web app, relocated off the root
  /docs/       documentation hub (src/web/docs/) linking to /docs/api + prose
  /download/   the desktop download page (unchanged location)

- The app moves to src/web/app/index.html with <base href="../"> so every
  relative asset/worker/seed URL still resolves to the shared root — no asset
  edits, correct under both Pages' /formal-ai/ prefix and the desktop static
  server. The desktop wrapper (desktop/main.cjs) now opens /app/index.html.
- The landing + docs pages render through a shared src/web/site-chrome.js
  chooser factory, reuse the Links-Notation preference store (theme + UI
  language) so a choice carries across every page, ship a strict same-origin
  CSP and a <noscript> fallback, and localize into en/ru/zh/hi.
- The /download back-link and the /tests diagnostics "Demo" link now point at
  ../app/; the e2e configs retarget baseURL to /app/ so app specs' relative
  goto('./') stay aimed at the app while connectivity reaches its sibling
  harness via ../tests/ and the landing/docs specs via ../ and ../docs/.
- release.yml's deploy-demo job generates the Rust API reference with
  `cargo doc --no-deps --lib` (lib name formal_ai, plus a root redirect so
  /docs/api/ doesn't 404) and copies it into the Pages artifact *after* the
  stamp step (rustdoc HTML has no version placeholders). Its timeout grows
  15→20m for the cold-cache compile; concurrency is made main-safe so a release
  run is never cancelled mid-flight. src/web/docs/api/ is gitignored.
- tests/e2e/tests/issue-479-site.spec.js covers both chooser pages (nav
  targets, app boot under base href, language/theme persistence, hidden version
  label on un-stamped builds); workflow_release.rs guards the new static + CI
  invariants and its existing guards are updated for the new layout.
commits.messageHeadline: feat(web): restructure site into landing/app/docs/download chooser (#…

commits.messageBody:
…ragment

CI's `cargo clippy --all-targets` fails under `-D warnings` on current stable
because newer clippy promoted two lints to deny-by-default. These were latent
on the branch (masked behind the first error) and block the PR's Lint job:

- src/agentic_coding/lexicon.rs: replace the manual `(len + 1) / 2` ceiling
  with `len.div_ceil(2)` (manual_div_ceil). div_ceil is stable since Rust 1.73,
  within the crate's 1.77 MSRV — the avoidance comment cited a stale 1.70 MSRV.
- tests/unit/ci-cd/desktop_release_resolve.rs: backtick GITHUB_TOKEN /
  GITHUB_OUTPUT in doc comments (doc_markdown) and allow
  literal_string_with_formatting_args on the mock-gh raw shell string, whose
  ${VAR} expansions look like format args but are deliberately plain shell.

Also add the changelog fragment for issue #479 (bump: minor) covering the
desktop-download fix, the screenshots, and the landing/app/docs/download
restructure.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(lint): satisfy clippy on newer stable + add issue-479 changelog f…

title: fix(desktop-release): restore desktop downloads + restructure site into landing/app/docs/download (#479)
